text:
Once upon a quiet summer evening, Daisy wandered through her cozy backyard. Her nose twitched upwards—there was something yummy fermenting somewhere. She peered inside the compost pile.

There! A squirming ball of yeast! Daisy grinned. That's always what I get from my homemade beer bread recipes.

She tucked it away carefully to ferment overnight. The next morning, she stirred it slowly. Lots of little bubbles popped. Soon enough, a bubbly tang filled the air.

"Ahhh!" she sighed. "What a party!"

Later, she sat outside and drank her beer bread cider. It fizzed with life. It felt right.

She'd do that again sometime. Maybe throw a little hops in too.





There. That should be enough fun for one day. She headed inside. Tomorrow was berry picking day. Sweet, juicy berries always made everything better.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
